 {"id":"bd-1ai1.5","title":"Define test strategy for embedded core + host bindings","description":"Specify unit and integration test strategy for future embedded core and each host-specific FFI crate (no implementation yet).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Define test strategy for embedded core + host bindings
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

- Define how to test an embedded core crate (pure logic + IO traits).
- Define per-host integration test expectations (WASM, C, Zig, JVM).
- Identify logging/diagnostics needs for FFI issues.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear mapping of test types to crates and build targets.

 {"id":"bd-1awh","title":"Implement Widget Introspection Debug Overlay tests","description":"# Implement Widget Introspection Debug Overlay tests

## Background
Part of Widget Introspection Debug Overlay (bd-17nn). Tests ensure debug overlay works correctly.

## Unit Tests
- overlay_shows_widget_hierarchy: Tree view displays correctly
- selection_highlights_widget: Selected widget is visually marked
- properties_panel_shows_correct_data: Widget properties displayed accurately
- toggle_on_off_works: F12 (or configured key) toggles overlay
- overlay_does_not_affect_underlying_render: Original content unchanged

## Integration Tests
- hover_over_widget_shows_inspector: Mouse over activates inspector
- inspector_traverses_widget_tree: Can navigate up/down hierarchy
- overlay_works_with_complex_layouts: Nested layouts handled correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests verify real widget introspection
- [ ] Performance overhead < 1ms when overlay disabled","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:45.595414674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:12:58.483153232Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:12:58.483135769Z","close_reason":"Added debug overlay unit tests in crates/ftui-widgets/src/debug_overlay.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1awh","depends_on_id":"bd-17nn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1b5h","title":"Demo Showcase: Live Log Search + Filter","description":"## Background
The log viewer is a core demo. Live search/filtering makes it feel powerful and real‑world.

## Scope / Goals
- Typeahead filtering with match highlighting
- Regex vs literal modes
- Case sensitivity toggle
- Optional context lines around matches
- Smooth behavior in inline mode (no flicker)

## Deliverables
- Search UI + filter pipeline
- Highlight spans in LogViewer
- Integration in demo showcase
- Tests: unit, snapshot, perf, PTY E2E
- Docs + demo script

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for filter correctness and highlights
- Snapshot tests for key states
- Perf tests with large logs (10k+ lines)
- PTY E2E with verbose logging (query + result counts)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search is fast and deterministic
- [ ] Highlighting accurate across modes
- [ ] Inline mode remains stable
- [ ] All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Demo Showcase: Live Log Search + Filter
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.

- The log viewer is a central demo. Live search and filtering makes it feel powerful and practical.

Goals
- Typeahead filtering with match highlighting.
- Toggle regex vs literal, case sensitivity, and "keep context" lines.
- Smooth behavior in inline mode (no flicker).

Testing
- Unit tests for filter correctness and highlight spans.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs showing typed queries and resulting output.

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.1","title":"Log Search spec + UX flow","description":"## UX / Behavior Spec
- Open search: `/` (consistent with vim-like search)
- Close: `Esc` (restores full log)
- Modes: Literal / Regex; Case Sensitive toggle
- Context lines: 0/1/2/5 lines around matches
- Empty query shows full log + hint
- No results state displays message + help

## Highlighting Rules
- All matches highlighted in list view
- Current match (if navigation added) is stronger highlight
- Highlights must not corrupt markup/ANSI

## Tests (Required)
- Unit: regex vs literal correctness
- Unit: case sensitivity toggle
- Snapshot: empty, matches, no results
- PTY E2E: open → type → toggle mode → exit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear behavior in all modes
- [ ] Highlighting is correct and legible
- [ ] Keybindings documented in help overlay

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.10","title":"Live Log Search — Accessibility/UX Review","description":"## Accessibility / UX Review Checklist

- Search input focus is obvious
- Highlighting uses shape/weight, not only color
- Contrast for highlight meets WCAG AA
- No flicker in inline mode while typing

## Tests
- Snapshot high‑contrast highlight
- PTY E2E: keyboard‑only use

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checklist items pass
- [ ] No usability regressions

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.11","title":"Live Log Search: Streaming Automaton + Incremental Updates","description":"Use automata/rolling hash to update matches incrementally instead of full scan.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Live Log Search: Streaming Automaton + Incremental Updates
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Flicker/Tear Detection Harness
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Correctness: add property tests for diff/present invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, no-ghosting on shrink)
- Golden outputs: checksum render outputs for canonical fixtures; ensure isomorphism under optimization
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.19","title":"Mux Compatibility Matrix + Fallback Tests","description":"Test tmux/screen/zellij fallbacks; verify safe overlay strategy with detailed logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Mux Compatibility Matrix + Fallback Tests
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2","title":"Alien Artifact: Formal Resize Scheduler Model","description":"## Goal
Design a mathematically principled resize scheduler (control theory + change-point detection + anytime-valid testing).

## Scope
- Formal model of resize event stream with uncertainty.
- Decision rules for when to coalesce vs render immediately.
- Explicit failure modes and safeguards.

## Deliverables
- Model spec with assumptions/priors and decision thresholds.
- Pseudocode for scheduler + evidence ledger.

## Tests / Validation
- Property tests for decision rule invariants.
- Simulation harness for synthetic resize streams.
- E2E logs include scheduler decisions and confidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scheduler is explainable and reproducible.
- Decision thresholds are defensible and documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22.885261170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:58:02.297481942Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:58:02.297460572Z","close_reason":"Completed spec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":331,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien Artifact Math Notes (MANDATORY)

Purpose
- Provide a mathematically principled resize scheduler with anytime validity, explicit loss tradeoffs, and provable bounds on worst‑case behavior.

Core Model
- Treat resize events as a non‑stationary time series with regime shifts.
- Objective: minimize expected loss L(action, state) with asymmetric costs.

Loss Matrix (example)
- keep_reflow | skip_frame
  - smooth (true)  : 0   |  30
  - storm (burst)  : 10  |  1
- Intent: during bursts, skipping a frame is cheap; during steady state, skipping is costly.

Decision Rule (Bayes)
- Maintain posterior over regimes:
  P(storm|data_t) ∝ P(storm) × Π P(x_i|storm)
- Action a* = argmin_a Σ_c L(a,c) × P(c|data_t)

Anytime Validity (e‑process)
- Define e‑value e_t such that E[e_t] ≤ 1 under null (no regime shift).
- Trigger “storm mode” when e_t ≥ 1/α (optional stopping safe).
- This lets the scheduler switch modes without invalidating guarantees.

Output Guarantees
- Bound expected latency under steady state.
- Bound average render cost under storms by throttling to max_fps.
- Explicitly log when bounds are violated.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","title":"Resize Scheduler: Change-Point Detection (BOCPD)","description":"## Task
Design a BOCPD-based regime detector for resize streams (burst vs steady states).

## Scope
- Define hazard function and run-length distribution.
- Output regime label + confidence for scheduler decisions.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests on synthetic streams (burst, periodic, noise).
- Simulation logs include detected change points and confidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detector is deterministic under fixed seed.
- Regime transitions are explainable and logged.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.343398831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:03:26.634191954Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:03:26.634171224Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":332,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BOCPD Details (Change‑Point Detection)

Model
- Observations: inter‑resize intervals Δt and magnitude |Δsize|.
- Assume piecewise‑stationary hazard λ (probability of regime change).
- Run length r_t tracks time since last change.

Recurrence
- p(r_t, x_t) = Σ_{r_{t-1}} p(r_{t-1}, x_{t-1}) × p(r_t|r_{t-1}) × p(x_t|r_{t-1})
- p(r_t=0|r_{t-1}) = λ (change)
- p(r_t=r_{t-1}+1|r_{t-1}) = 1-λ (no change)

Outputs
- Posterior P(r_t=0|x_1:t) gives change‑point probability.
- Use threshold τ_cp to enter storm mode.

Parameters
- λ chosen from empirical resize patterns (configurable, safe default).
- Likelihood modeled with exponential/gamma on Δt, mixture for |Δsize|.

Guarantees
- Sensitivity to burst onset; bounded false positives via λ and τ_cp.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:11Z"},{"id":333,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started.
